title: add-jwt-oidc-env-defaults
summary: Added centralized JWT/OIDC auth environment defaults (`VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT`, `VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE`, `VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE`, `VSO_OIDC_ISSUER`, `VSO_OIDC_JWKS_URL`) to `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh`, preserved legacy Kubernetes-auth vars for migration compatibility, updated the environment summary helper, and added a new validation test suite proving defaults/overrides/no-unbound-variable behavior (19/19 passing, all other existing test suites still pass).
keyLearnings: - Defaults chosen per task spec/spike: `VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT=jwt-vso`, `VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE=vso-demo`, `VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE=vault`, `VSO_OIDC_ISSUER=https://kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local` (the JWT's real `iss` claim â€” `bound_issuer` is a string compare, not a fetch URL), `VSO_OIDC_JWKS_URL=${VSO_API_ADDR}/openid/v1/jwks` (derived from the reachable cross-cluster API address).

- The plan doc's original Phase 3 draft had `VSO_OIDC_ISSUER`/`VSO_OIDC_JWKS_URL` backwards (issuer set to the reachable host, jwks derived from issuer) â€” corrected per task 01's spike decision and annotated with a status note so future readers don't reintroduce the bug.

- Legacy `VSO_AUTH_MOUNT`, `VSO_AUTH_ROLE`, `VAULT_TOKEN_REVIEWER_SA` are untouched and explicitly documented as migration-compatibility holdovers â€” do not remove until all downstream tasks (04-11) have migrated off `auth/kubernetes-vso`.

- All new vars follow the existing `"${VAR:-default}"` override pattern, so they work with both plain env-var overrides and `make setup VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE=...`-style command-line overrides (same mechanism the Makefile already relies on for `VAULT_CONTEXT`/`VSO_CONTEXT`).

- Noticed task 02 (kind OIDC issuer config) is running concurrently in the same working tree (uncommitted changes to `scripts/create-clusters.sh`, `scripts/kind/vso-lab-config.yaml.tmpl`, new `test-vso-lab-kind-config-validation.sh`, `docs/vso-jwt-oidc-auth-task-02.md`). Verified no file/section overlap with my task 03 edits before finishing.

- Downstream tasks (04+) should consume `VSO_JWT_AUTH_MOUNT`/`VSO_JWT_AUTH_ROLE`/`VSO_JWT_AUDIENCE`/`VSO_OIDC_ISSUER`/`VSO_OIDC_JWKS_URL` directly from this shared lib rather than redeclaring defaults.
blockers: none â€” all acceptance/validation criteria for task 03 met; task 04's RBAC gap (`oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/Binding) from the task 01 spike is still open but explicitly out of scope for task 03 (belongs to task 04/05).
